2606.28217.title: Towards Value-Constrained Credit Assignment in Fully Delegated AI Cooperatives
2606.28217.paper_summary: We propose a framework for reward allocation in fully delegated AI cooperatives where humans are represented by agents that contribute data and participate in model updates under heterogeneous value constraints. The key idea is to credit only those updates that remain admissible after screening them against each principal's value profile. We formulate value-conditioned gradient filtering, online marginal contribution signals, and cumulative revenue settlement within a traversal learning (TL) substrate. TL is especially attractive here because it performs decentralized backpropagation without the quality loss associated with aggregation-centric distributed learning and, we argue, offers a finer attribution substrate than FedAvg-style federated learning by preserving explicit traversal and gradient paths. The framework is positioned against data valuation, federated contribution estimation, personalized federated learning, and pluralistic alignment.
2606.28217.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個框架，用於完全委託的人工智慧合作社中的獎勵分配，在這裡人類由貢獻數據並參與模型更新的代理表示，並在異質價值約束下運作。<lb/>關鍵思想是僅對那些在根據每位委託人的價值概況進行篩選後仍然可接受的更新進行信用。<lb/>我們制定了基於價值的梯度過濾、在線邊際貢獻信號和累積收益結算，這些都在遍歷學習（TL）基底中進行。<lb/>在這裡，TL 特別有吸引力，因為它執行去中心化的反向傳播，而不會因為以聚合為中心的分佈式學習而導致質量損失，並且我們認為，通過保留明確的遍歷和梯度路徑，它提供了比 FedAvg 風格的聯邦學習更精細的歸因基底。<lb/>該框架與數據估值、聯邦貢獻估計、個性化聯邦學習和多元對齊相對應。
2606.28217.author: Young Yoon et.al.
2606.28217.authors: Young Yoon, Jimin Kim, Soyeon Park
